messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI 3.0 – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI 3.0 – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance to join our 1-day seminar Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI 3.0 livestreaming March 2 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Group Leader (PI) at LIOS). This seminar explores deep learning techniques that are highly applicable for analyzing the complex, often time-series or sequential, datasets common in epidemiological research. Participants will gain practical skills in R and Python, using tools like TensorFlow and Keras, and leverage ChatGPT to develop and fine-tune neural network architectures for tasks such as disease outbreak prediction or risk factor identification. These advanced methods will enable researchers to uncover intricate patterns, perform sophisticated dimensionality reduction, and quantify uncertainties in their epidemiological models, thereby enhancing the rigor and insight of their studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with-4) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Bayesian Modelling Using R & INLA - Dr Virgilio Gómez-Rubio
messages.subject_clean: Bayesian Modelling Using R & INLA - Dr Virgilio Gómez-Rubio
messages.body_plain:
Advance your statistical modelling skills with our live online course *Bayesian
Modelling Using R & INLA (BMIN04)*.

https://prstats.org/course/bayesian-modelling-using-r-inla-bmin04/

Instructor - Dr Virgilio G?mez-Rubio, a lead expert in the application of
INLA and the author of 'Bayesian Inference with INLA'.

This applied training provides a practical introduction to Bayesian
inference using the Integrated Nested Laplace Approximation (INLA)
framework in R. Participants will learn both conceptual foundations and
hands-on implementation of Bayesian models for complex data structures,
with workflows suitable for spatial, temporal, and hierarchical analyses.

The course covers:

   -

   Bayesian fundamentals and interpretation
   -

   Specification and fitting of Bayesian models in R using INLA
   -

   Model selection, comparison, and diagnostics
   -

   Handling spatial, temporal, and spatio-temporal data
   -

   Hierarchical and mixed-effects modelling in a Bayesian framework
   -

   Practical strategies for reproducible Bayesian workflows

Delivered live online, this course offers direct interaction with the
instructor, opportunities to ask questions about your own data, and
structured guidance through practical examples. All participants receive
course materials, example code, and post-course support.

*Course details*
Dates: 4-8 May 2026
Duration: 5 days (approximately 7 hours per day)
Format: Live online
Fee: ?500

This course is suitable for postgraduate students, researchers, analysts,
and professionals interested in applying Bayesian methods to real data
using R and INLA.

Full details and registration:
https://prstats.org/course/bayesian-modelling-using-r-inla-bmin04/
Email oliver at prstats.org with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI 3.0 – livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI 3.0 – livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Instats is offering a new 1-day seminar Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with AI 3.0 livestreaming March 25, led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Group Leader (PI) at LIOS). This seminar explores deep learning techniques for analyzing complex datasets, offering powerful methods to uncover intricate patterns in large-scale epidemiological information. Participants will learn to apply convolutional and recurrent neural networks to sequential or time-series epidemiological data, and utilize autoencoders for dimensionality reduction or clustering of complex health outcomes. The practical skills gained in R, Python, TensorFlow, and Keras, enhanced by AI assistance, can be directly integrated into your existing research workflows to develop more sophisticated predictive and analytical models.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with-4) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Bayesian Statistical Modelling with Stan and brms
messages.subject_clean: Bayesian Statistical Modelling with Stan and brms
messages.body_plain:
Develop advanced Bayesian modelling skills in our live online course *Bayesian
Statistical Modelling with Stan and brms (BMSB01)*.

This hands-on workshop provides a comprehensive introduction to Bayesian
data analysis using *Stan* via the *brms*package in R. Participants learn
both the theoretical foundations of Bayesian inference and practical
workflows for fitting and interpreting Bayesian models with real datasets.

The course covers the full Bayesian modelling workflow, including how to
define priors, fit models, evaluate convergence, and interpret posterior
distributions.

*The course covers:*

   -

   Foundations of Bayesian reasoning and statistical inference
   -

   Fitting Bayesian regression models in R using the *brms* interface to
   Stan
   -

   Understanding likelihoods, priors, and posterior distributions
   -

   Model comparison and Bayesian diagnostics
   -

   Interpreting MCMC output (trace plots, R-hat, effective sample size)
   -

   Extending models to generalized linear and hierarchical models

Through practical coding exercises, participants gain experience
implementing Bayesian models and interpreting results in a modern
statistical workflow.

Delivered live online, the course allows direct interaction with the
instructor, opportunities to ask questions, and discussion of your own
modelling challenges. All participants receive course materials, example
code, and post-course support.

*Course details*
Dates: 5?7 May 2026
Duration: 3 days (approximately 6 hours per day)
Format: Live online
Fee: ?400

This course is suitable for postgraduate students, researchers, and
analysts who want to apply Bayesian methods to real datasets using R and
Stan.

Full details and registration:
https://prstats.org/course/bayesian-statistical-modelling-with-stan-and-brms-bmsb01/
Email oliver at prstats.org with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Robust Survey Design for Epidemiological Research
messages.subject_clean: Robust Survey Design for Epidemiological Research
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

This seminar provides practical skills to elevate the rigor and reliability of your empirical studies. Introduction to Survey Design is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 20?21, led by Adam Berinsky (MIT Political Experiments Research Lab, Massachusetts Institute of Technology). This seminar offers essential principles and practical techniques for robust survey design, crucial for generating high-quality data in epidemiological studies. Researchers can apply these methods to improve measurement validity in their own surveys, ensuring that data collected for R-based statistical analysis accurately reflects health behaviors, risk factors, or patient outcomes. Mastering these skills directly enhances the credibility and replicability of findings, strengthening empirical evidence for publications and grant proposals in public health and epidemiology.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-survey-design) to reserve your place, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Analyzing Intensive Longitudinal Data in R
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Analyzing Intensive Longitudinal Data in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - This seminar will equip you with robust methods for analyzing within-person processes in your research. Analyzing Intensive Longitudinal Data 2.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 9?10 with Scott Colwell (University of Guelph and Enablytics). Intensive longitudinal data methods, taught with R, enable epidemiologists to analyze within-person processes that drive health outcomes, disease progression, or intervention responses over time. Researchers can apply these advanced techniques to model temporal dynamics, momentary predictors of behavior, and contextual influences on individual health trajectories, moving beyond cross-sectional or sparsely sampled designs. This workshop equips you with practical skills in data wrangling, multilevel modeling, and dynamic analyses in R, providing reproducible pipelines and APA-ready templates to enhance your epidemiological research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/analyzing-intensive-longitudinal-data-20) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] (Online Course) Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R - 13–17 April
messages.subject_clean: (Online Course) Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R - 13–17 April
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
Are youunning clinical studies, epidemiology projects, or biostatistical analyses?but want stronger statistical foundations? 
 
We are pleased to announce the upcoming Physalia online course Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R, taking place 13?17 April.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ ) 
 

This 5-day course provides a practical introduction to the core statistical concepts and methods used in medical and biomedical research. Through hands-on exercises in R, participants will learn how to explore datasets, choose appropriate statistical tests, and correctly interpret results.

Topics include:

1 - Descriptive statistics and data visualization

2 - Probability and confidence intervals

3 - Hypothesis testing (t-tests, ANOVA, non-parametric tests)

4 - Categorical data analysis and regression models

5 - Logistic regression and survival analysis

6 - Reproducible statistical workflows for research publications

The course is ideal for researchers, clinicians, and students who want to strengthen their statistical foundations and improve their data analysis skills using R.


Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: AI for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analysis
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: AI for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Explore rigorous AI-integrated workflows to modernize your approach to evidence synthesis. AI for Systematic Reviews is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 11?12 with Piers Steel (Haskayne School of Business, University of Calgary) and Hadi Fariborzi (Bissett School of Business, Mount Royal University, Calgary). The increasing volume of epidemiological literature makes traditional review methods unsustainable; this seminar introduces rigorous AI-integrated workflows to manage scale without sacrificing validity in your research. You will learn to embed artificial intelligence into every stage of the evidence synthesis pipeline, with practical demonstrations in HubMeta and R to master key quantitative workflows for your epidemiological data. This training will enable you to create analysis-ready datasets, conduct advanced quantitative meta-analyses, and perform bias diagnostics, directly enhancing the reproducibility and rigor of your own studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-for-systematic-reviews) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: RNA-seq: From FASTQ to Counts
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: RNA-seq: From FASTQ to Counts
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Accurate and reproducible processing of RNA-seq data is fundamental for advanced statistical analysis in your field. RNA-seq: From FASTQ to Counts is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 23 and 25 with Estefania Mancini (Josep Carreras Leukaemia Research Institute). RNA sequencing provides critical molecular insights that can inform epidemiological studies, but processing raw data into reliable formats for analysis is complex. This seminar teaches how to implement reproducible nf-core Nextflow pipelines to transform FASTQ files into high-quality read count matrices. Mastering these data processing workflows will enable you to generate robust inputs for your R-based statistical and epidemiological analyses.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/rna-seq-from-fastq-to-counts) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Integrating Large Language Model Tools into R Workflows (Online course)
messages.subject_clean: Integrating Large Language Model Tools into R Workflows (Online course)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We would like to inform you that only a few seats remain available for our upcoming online workshop: Integrating Large Language Model Tools into R Workflows
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/llms-4-r-mncn-csic/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/llms-4-r-mncn-csic/ ) 


Date: 25 March | 13:00?18:00 (Berlin time)
 
This one-day course is organized by Physalia-courses in conjunction with the 9th Scientific Conference of the National Museum of Natural Sciences (MNCN-CSIC).
During this hands-on workshop, participants will learn how Large Language Models (LLMs) can be integrated directly into R-based data analysis workflows. The course will demonstrate practical ways to generate, edit, document, and troubleshoot R code using LLM-assisted tools, helping researchers work more efficiently and reproducibly.
 
Topics include:

Setting up and using LLM assistants in RStudio and Positron

Generating and editing R code with natural language prompts

Creating data visualizations with LLM support

Extracting structured data from PDFs using ellmer

Best practices for responsible and reproducible use of AI tools in research
This workshop is designed for researchers and R users with basic programming experience. No prior knowledge of LLMs is required.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Agentic Data Analytics for Research Automation
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Agentic Data Analytics for Research Automation
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - This seminar explores innovative methods to automate complex data tasks and accelerate your research findings. Agentic Data Analytics: Automating Research Workflows is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 24 and 27 with Peter Gruber (Faculty of Economics, USI Universit? della Svizzera Italiana). Agentic AI offers a powerful new paradigm for researchers, enabling the automation of entire scientific workflows from data cleaning to report generation. For those working with epidemiological data in R, this approach can significantly streamline tedious tasks like data wrangling, statistical modeling, and creating publication-ready visualizations and LaTeX reports. By utilizing tools like Google Antigravity to run advanced AI agents locally, researchers can enhance their analytical efficiency while maintaining privacy for sensitive health datasets, directly advancing their epidemiological studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/agentic-data-analytics-automating-resear) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] (Online course) Network Analysis in R - 4–7 May
messages.subject_clean: (Online course) Network Analysis in R - 4–7 May
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce our upcoming online course: Network Analysis in R, taking place 4?7 May.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/network-analysis-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/network-analysis-in-r/ )
 
This 4-day course guides participants through the full workflow of network analysis in R?from creating and exploring network objects, calculating statistics, detecting clusters, and statistical modeling, to advanced network visualization. Each day blends theory with hands-on live coding, giving you practical experience you can immediately apply to your research.
 
The course is designed for researchers with intermediate R skills and an interest in analysing networks in the life sciences?or any field where networks are relevant.
 
For those interested in systems biology applications, we also offer a companion course on Network Analysis in Systems Biology with R/Bioconductor: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/network-in-systems-biology/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/network-in-systems-biology/ ) 
 
Best regards, Carlo

--------------------

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: AI for Clinical Reasoning and Diagnosis
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: AI for Clinical Reasoning and Diagnosis
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Explore new methods for AI-driven clinical reasoning and diagnostic support. AI for Clinical Reasoning and Diagnosis (Free Seminar) is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 25 with Balu Bhasuran (School of Information (iSchool), Florida State University). This seminar examines AI-driven clinical reasoning by integrating laboratory results, EHRs, and large language models with causal inference. It provides methods for constructing causal graphs, performing counterfactual evaluations, and building reproducible pipelines for diagnostic hypotheses and patient explanations. Researchers can apply these robust evaluation strategies and data integration techniques to advance their epidemiological analyses and clinical research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-for-clinical-reasoning-and-diagnosis) to register now, and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] (Online course) Species Distribution Modeling with BART (1–3 July)
messages.subject_clean: (Online course) Species Distribution Modeling with BART (1–3 July)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
Do you already have some experience with species distribution modeling and want to learn a modern, powerful method to take your analyses further?
 
Join our online course, Species Distribution Modeling with BART (1?3 July) and gain hands-on experience with:

Building and evaluating BART models in R for species occurrence

Selecting informative predictors using embarcadero and dbarts

Visualizing and interpreting effects and interactions in your models

Projecting species distributions into new regions or times
Each day combines live sessions with guided practical exercises and live support to make sure you can
 
If interested, check it out: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/barts/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/barts/ ) 
 
Best regards, Carlo

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: R Programming for Statistics and Machine Learning
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: R Programming for Statistics and Machine Learning
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Discover powerful R programming techniques for advanced statistical analysis and machine learning in your research. R Programming for Statistics and Machine Learning is a 2-day seminar livestreaming March 27 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Group Leader (PI) at LIOS). This seminar explores R programming as an integrated toolkit for contemporary statistical inference and machine learning, covering methods from classical linear modeling to modern dimensionality reduction and clustering. Epidemiologists can apply these advanced R programming skills to analyze complex high-dimensional biological, clinical, or observational data, enhancing model selection, feature engineering, and transparent reporting in their research. Mastering these techniques will empower researchers to design, implement, and report sophisticated analyses, improving the credibility and impact of their peer-reviewed publications.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/r-programming-for-statistics-and-machine) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
